mnemonics.title: Markov Memoryless
mnemonics.content: M-A-R-K-O-V: Memoryless - Absent - Records - Keeping - Only - Present matters. Future depends only on present state, not history.

mnemonics.title: Transient vs Recurrent
mnemonics.content: T-RANS - T-RECUR: Transient = finite visits, Recurrent = infinite visits. Think 'T' for 'Terminates' (finite) and 'R' for 'Returns forever'.

mnemonics.title: Stationary Distribution
mnemonics.content: S-T-E-A-D-Y: Sum = 1, Transition preserves, Equation π = πP, Always exists for ergodic chains, Deterministic long-term, Your limiting probability.

acronyms.usage: Used to compute multi-step transition probabilities by breaking into smaller steps: P(m+n) = P(m) × P(n)
Standard queue model with Poisson arrivals and exponential service times

acronyms.expansion: Probability of transition from state i to state j in one step
acronyms.usage: Entry in row i, column j of transition matrix P

acronyms.expansion: n-step transition probability from i to j
acronyms.usage: Entry in row i, column j of matrix P^n

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a grid/table with states as row/column headers. Each cell shows arrow from row state to column state with probability. All arrows from a row should sum to 1.

Use a directed graph with states as nodes and edges showing transitions. Absorbing states have self-loops with probability 1. Recurrent states form cycles. Transient states lead to other states but don't return.
Think of water flowing through pipes. Steady-state is when water flow rate into each state equals flow rate out, making levels constant. π shows the 'water level' in each state.
Imagine a maze where from any room you can eventually reach any other room. After enough steps (power of matrix), you have positive probability to be anywhere.

analogies.analogy: Like predicting weather where today's weather depends only on yesterday's, not on the whole history. Today being 'Sunny' predicts tomorrow's weather the same way whether it was sunny 5 days ago or rainy.
Think of a random web surfer clicking links randomly. PageRank is the probability of finding the surfer on each page after infinite clicking - pages with more/incoming links get higher rank.
Customers arriving like raindrops and being served like evaporating drops. The queue length fluctuates but in the long run reaches a balanced distribution.
A drunk person stumbling left or right at each step. The next step direction doesn't depend on previous steps - just the current position determines everything.
